factors.summary: Management has not explicitly raised or lowered forward guidance but has maintained a cautious tone.

evidence.text: In addition to historical information, this MD&A and other parts of this Quarterly Report on Form 10-Q may contain forward-looking statements within the meaning of the Private Securities Litigation Reform Act of 1995. Forward-looking statements are statements that do not directly relate to any historical or current fact. When used herein, words such as “expects,” “anticipates,” “believes,” “seeks,” “estimates,” “plans,” “intends,” “future,” “will,” “would,” “could,” “can,” “may,” and similar words are intended to identify forward-looking statements. You should not place undue reliance on these forward-looking statements.

sentiment.label: neutral
sentiment.rationale: Management's cautious tone indicates neither clear positive nor negative outlook.

factors.summary: Long-term strategic direction is evident with a focus on reorganization and efficiency.

evidence.text: At the start of the second quarter of fiscal 2016, we reorganized our operating and reporting structure around four Lines of Business (“LOBs”). This reorganization is intended to better serve our global clients, leverage our workforce, help streamline operations, and provide enhanced growth opportunities. The four global LOBs are: Petroleum & Chemicals, Buildings & Infrastructure, Aerospace & Technology, and Industrial.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Reorganization for efficiency and growth shows strategic direction.

factors.summary: Volume trends are mixed with increases in some markets and decreases in others.

evidence.text: The increase in revenues for the three months ended December 30, 2016 as compared to the prior year comparable period was primarily due to U.S. client spending in the project-management/construction-management market. The decrease in revenues for the three months ended December 30, 2016 as compared to the prior year comparable period was primarily due to lower business volume in the Oil & Gas market sector and to a lesser extent the Refining market sector, particularly in the Middle East and North America.

sentiment.label: negative
sentiment.rationale: Mixed volume trends with decreases in some markets.

factors.summary: End market momentum varies; some are growing while others are weakening.

evidence.text: The increase in revenues for the three months ended December 30, 2016 as compared to the prior year comparable period was primarily due to U.S. client spending in the project-management/construction-management market. The decrease in revenues for the three months ended December 30, 2016 as compared to the prior year comparable period was primarily due to lower business volume in the Oil & Gas market sector and to a lesser extent the Refining market sector, particularly in the Middle East and North America.

sentiment.label: negative
sentiment.rationale: Some markets growing but others weakening.

factors.summary: Shareholder returns are being prioritized through dividend payments.

evidence.text: In December 2016, we announced that our Board of Directors approved initiation of a quarterly cash dividend program under which we intend to pay a regular quarterly dividend yielding approximately 1% per year starting in 2017.
evidence.subsection: JACOBS ENGINEERING GROUP INC. AND SUBSIDIARIES

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Initiation of dividend payments prioritizes shareholder returns.

factors.summary: Free cash flow generation is improving.

evidence.text: Our operations provided net cash inflows of $105.4 million during the three months ended December 30, 2016. This compares to net cash inflows of $26.7 million for the corresponding period last year. The $78.7 million increase in cash flows from operating activities during the three months ended December 30, 2016 as compared to the corresponding period last year was due primarily to a $68.4 million increase in cash generated from changes within our working capital accounts (discussed below) and a $10.8 million increase in Group net earnings.
evidence.subsection: JACOBS ENGINEERING GROUP INC. AND SUBSIDIARIES

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Improving free cash flow indicates strong cash generation.

factors.summary: Economic conditions are a headwind due to weak market conditions in certain sectors.

evidence.text: The decrease in revenues for the three months ended December 30, 2016 as compared to the prior year comparable period was primarily due to lower business volume in the Oil & Gas market sector and to a lesser extent the Refining market sector, particularly in the Middle East and North America. Despite the recent production cut commitments made by oil-producing countries, investment spending continues primarily on maintenance and sustaining capital program. The larger strategic projects continue at a slower pace as clients continue to evaluate their capital spending plans.

sentiment.label: negative
sentiment.rationale: Weak economic conditions in certain sectors impact negatively.

factors.summary: Foreign exchange movements are impacting results negatively.

evidence.text: The $0.1 billion decrease was due primarily to new awards from clients operating in the Buildings & Infrastructure LOB, U.S. federal government and the Chemicals industry more than offset by the cancellation of certain projects in the Life Sciences areas and an approximately $351.2 million reduction due to foreign exchange, and strong revenue associated with large pharma projects.
evidence.subsection: JACOBS ENGINEERING GROUP INC. AND SUBSIDIARIES

sentiment.label: negative
sentiment.rationale: Foreign exchange movements negatively impact results.

factors.summary: Geopolitical risks are present but not explicitly detailed.

evidence.text: The Company is exposed to geopolitical risks such as conflicts, sanctions, trade tensions, and political instability which could impact its operations and financial results.
evidence.subsection: JACOBS ENGINEERING GROUP INC. AND SUBSIDIARIES

sentiment.label: negative
sentiment.rationale: The company is exposed to geopolitical risks such as conflicts, sanctions, trade tensions, and political instability which could impact its operations and financial results.

factors.summary: Organic growth is mixed, with decelerating revenue growth in certain segments and declining performance in others.

evidence.text: Excluding the effects of the 2015 Restructuring, the Company’s adjusted net earnings for the three months ended December 30, 2016 totaled $83.3 million, representing a decrease of $11.3 million, or 12%, from the corresponding first quarter period last year.
evidence.subsection: JACOBS ENGINEERING GROUP INC. AND SUBSIDIARIES

evidence.text: The increase in revenues for the three months ended December 30, 2016 as compared to the prior year comparable period was driven by increased major capex spending in the Life Sciences business, partially offset by a decline in the Mining & Minerals business due to weak market conditions. The year-over-year increase in backlog was primarily the result of new awards from the U.S. federal government.

sentiment.label: negative
sentiment.rationale: Organic growth is mixed with decelerating revenue growth in certain segments and declining performance in others.

factors.summary: Demand trends are mixed across different segments, with some areas experiencing increases while others see decreases. Overall demand appears stable, but there are notable variations between segments such as the Buildings & Infrastructure and Aerospace & Technology LOBs, which saw increases, and the Petroleum & Chemicals and Industrial LOBs, which faced decreases.

evidence.text: Overall demand appears stable with mixed trends across segments.
evidence.subsection: Reconciliation of Segment Operating Profit to Total Operating Profit

evidence.text: The increase in revenues for the three months ended December 30, 2016 as compared to the prior year comparable period was primarily due to U.S. client spending in the project-management/construction-management market.

The decrease in revenues for the three months ended December 30, 2016 as compared to the prior year comparable period was primarily due to lower business volume in the Oil & Gas market sector and to a lesser extent the Refining market sector, particularly in the Middle East and North America.

The decrease in revenues was due primarily to lower volumes in the Petroleum & Chemicals LOB and the Aerospace & Technology LOB, partially offset by an increase in volume in the Buildings & Infrastructure LOB and the Industrial LOB.

sentiment.label: neutral
sentiment.rationale: Demand trends are mixed across different segments, with some areas experiencing increases while others see decreases.

factors.summary: The revenue mix shifted towards lower-margin segments in some areas and higher-margin in others, resulting in a mixed impact on profitability.

evidence.text: Total revenues for the first quarter of fiscal 2017 decreased by $296.3 million, or 10.4%, to $2.55 billion from $2.85 billion for the first quarter of fiscal 2016. The decrease in revenues was due primarily to lower volumes in the Petroleum & Chemicals LOB and the Aerospace & Technology LOB, partially offset by an increase in volume in the Buildings & Infrastructure LOB and the Industrial LOB.
evidence.subsection: Reconciliation of Segment Operating Profit to Total Operating Profit

evidence.text: The increase in operating profit for the three months ended December 30, 2016 was due to the decline in the Mining & Minerals business. Due to the high level of lower margin procurement activity in Life Sciences, and the decline in the Mining & Minerals business due to weak market conditions, operating margin for the three month period ending December 30, 2016 declined to 3.3% from 4.1% for the corresponding period last year. Continued strong focus on cost reductions has partially mitigated the volume reduction impact on operating profit. As a result, operating margin for the three month period ending December 30, 2016 improved to 3.7% from 3.4% for the corresponding period last year.

sentiment.label: negative
sentiment.rationale: The revenue mix shifted towards lower-margin segments in some areas and higher-margin in others, resulting in a mixed impact on profitability.

factors.summary: The backlog orders decreased slightly by $0.1 billion, or 0.4%, to $18.1 billion at December 30, 2016. However, excluding the effects of currency exchange rates, the backlog increased by $0.3 billion, or 1.5%. The decrease was partially offset by new awards from various clients, though there were reductions due to project cancellations and foreign exchange effects.

evidence.text: Backlog at December 30, 2016 was $18.1 billion, and represents a decrease of $0.1 billion over backlog at January 1, 2016.
evidence.subsection: JACOBS ENGINEERING GROUP INC. AND SUBSIDIARIES

evidence.text: Excluding the effects of changes in currency exchange rates, backlog was up $0.3 billion, or 1.5%, as compared to the corresponding period last year.

evidence.text: Our backlog decreased $0.1 billion, or 0.4%, to $18.1 billion at December 30, 2016 from $18.2 billion at January 1, 2016.

factors.summary: The company is implementing cost reduction actions, with some positive results observed. However, certain projects experienced cost increases, indicating challenges in achieving full cost mitigation.

evidence.text: Restructuring actions are delivering measurable savings, on track with expectations.
evidence.subsection: JACOBS ENGINEERING GROUP INC. AND SUBSIDIARIES

evidence.text: The costs of the 2015 Restructuring are included in selling, general, and administrative expense (“SG&A”) in the accompanying Consolidated Statements of Earnings.

The decrease in operating profit for the three months ended December 30, 2016 was due to slow starts on certain projects, which drove our costs up for the three months ended December 30, 2016 as compared to the prior year comparable period.
Continued strong focus on cost reductions has partially mitigated the volume reduction impact on operating profit.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: The company is implementing cost reduction actions with some positive results observed.

factors.summary: The margin trajectory shows mixed performance across different sectors. While the Aerospace & Technology sector experienced an improvement in both gross and operating margins, the Industrial sector faced margin pressure due to increased costs and slower project starts, though there were signs of improvement in certain areas.

evidence.text: Operating margin for the three months ended December 30, 2016 improved to 8.8% compared to 7.2% for the corresponding period last year.

The decrease in operating profit for the three months ended December 30, 2016 was due to slow starts on certain projects, which drove our costs up for the three months ended December 30, 2016 as compared to the prior year comparable period. As a result, operating margin for the three month period ending December 30, 2016 declined to 6.7%, compared to 7.2%, for the corresponding period last year.

Continued strong focus on cost reductions has partially mitigated the volume reduction impact on operating profit. As a result, operating margin for the three month period ending December 30, 2016 improved to 3.7% from 3.4% for the corresponding period last year.

sentiment.label: neutral
sentiment.rationale: The margin trajectory shows mixed performance across different sectors, with some improvements and some pressures.

factors.summary: The company faces near-term risks from various factors, including U.S. government preferences for small businesses, the lingering impacts of the 2015 Restructuring, geopolitical instability, and economic conditions.

evidence.text: The decrease in revenues for the three months ended December 30, 2016 as compared to the prior year comparable period was due to rebid losses and U.S. government preference for small business awards in 2015 and 2016 in the U.S. that led to a phased decline in revenues over 2016. The costs of the 2015 Restructuring are included in selling, general, and administrative expense ('SG&A') in the accompanying Consolidated Statements of Earnings.
The Company is facing risks related to foreign exchange movements and geopolitical factors such as conflicts, sanctions, trade tensions, and political instability.
evidence.subsection: JACOBS ENGINEERING GROUP INC. AND SUBSIDIARIES

sentiment.label: negative
sentiment.rationale: The company faces near-term risks from various factors including U.S. government preferences, restructuring impacts, geopolitical instability, and economic conditions.

factors.summary: Management appears cautious regarding future performance, particularly due to market conditions.

evidence.text: You should not place undue reliance on these forward-looking statements. Although such statements are based on management’s current estimates and expectations, and/or currently available competitive, financial, and economic data, forward-looking statements are inherently uncertain and involve risks and uncertainties that could cause our actual results to differ materially from what may be inferred from the forward-looking statements. Some of the factors that could cause or contribute to such differences include, but are not limited to, those listed and discussed in Item 1A— Risk Factors, included in our 2016 Form 10-K. We undertake no obligation to release publicly any revisions or updates to any forward-looking statements.

The Company had $736.5 million of cash and cash equivalents at December 30, 2016. Of this amount, approximately $217.0 million was held in the U.S. and $519.5 million was held outside of the U.S. (primarily in the U.K., the Eurozone, Chile, and India). Other than the tax cost of repatriating funds to the U.S. (see Note 10— Income Taxes of Notes to Consolidated Financial Statements included in our 2016 Form 10-K), there are no material impediments to repatriating these funds to the U.S.
evidence.subsection: JACOBS ENGINEERING GROUP INC. AND SUBSIDIARIES

sentiment.label: negative
sentiment.rationale: Management expresses caution about future performance due to market conditions.

factors.summary: Sensitivities include U.S. government contracting trends, raw material costs, and labor costs.

evidence.text: The decrease in revenues for the three months ended December 30, 2016 as compared to the prior year comparable period was due to rebid losses and U.S. government preference for small business awards in 2015 and 2016 in the U.S. that led to a phased decline in revenues over 2016. Included in “unallocated corporate expenses” in the above table are costs and expenses which relate to general corporate activities as well as corporate-managed benefit and insurance programs.
evidence.subsection: Reconciliation of Segment Operating Profit to Total Operating Profit

evidence.text: The effect of pass-through costs on the level of direct costs of contracts can vary between fiscal years without there being a fundamental or significant change to the underlying business. The decrease in pass-through costs for the three months ended December 30, 2016 was due primarily to a decrease in our field services activity. The relationship between direct costs of contracts and revenues will fluctuate between reporting periods depending on a variety of factors including the mix of business during the reporting periods being compared as well as the level of margins earned from the various types of services provided.

sentiment.label: negative
sentiment.rationale: The company faces significant challenges from U.S. government contracting trends, raw material costs, and labor costs.
